Entity2: Protocol (CONCEPT).

Sentence: Transport Layer • Takes data from higher levels of OSI Model and breaks it into segments that can be sent to lower-level layers for data transmission • Conversely, reassembles data segments into data that higher-level protocols and applications can use • Also puts segments in correct order (called sequencing ) so they can be reassembled in correct order at destination • Concerned with the reliability of the transport of sent data • May use a connection-oriented protocol such as TCP to ensure destination received segments • May use a connectionless protocol such as UDP to send segments without assurance of delivery • Uses port addressing Entity1: OSI Model (TOPIC). Entity2: Transport Layer (TOPIC).
